prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: Each night the keeper climbed spiral stairs to the lamp and watched the sea learn itself in fog. He measured breath with his barometer, cataloged gulls’ tired shapes against the lantern’s halo and polished routines until they glowed. One morning when fog was milk and the world muffled, something not of sea rolled up and settled at the cliff’s foot: a piano, black and salt-crusted, lid ajar like an offering. He ran fingers along keys that had not known his hands; they hummed, not with music but with memory — wind-pressed chords of storms, the weight of distant harbor lights. He set a lamp beside the instrument and watched mist rearrange itself into shorelines he had only read on maps. Hours later the piano, emptied of whatever tide had given it, slid away with the incoming fog, leaving an ivory key among the rocks. He could not explain the feeling.
